mnemonics.title: CNF Conversion Order
mnemonics.content: EVERY SANDWICH ULTIMATELY NEEDS CHEESE: Eliminate ε → Remove unit → Remove useless → Split long → Terminal to variable → Convert to CNF (2-symbol RHS)

mnemonics.title: CNF Rules Remember
mnemonics.content: TWO Rule: Terminals alone OR Two Variables; NO to ε, units, and S-on-RHS

acronyms.usage: Standardized form for context-free grammars used in parsing algorithms
Algorithm for membership testing requiring CNF grammar
Grammar type that CNF applies to
Another normal form where RHS starts with a terminal
Represents the empty string in formal language theory

visualTips.visualization: Think of CNF as binary trees: A → BC is like joining two branches, A → a is a leaf node. Only two shapes allowed: a single leaf (terminal) or a binary fork (two variables).
Picture nullable variables as 'ghost' symbols that can disappear. For each production, imagine removing nullable variables in all possible combinations to create new productions.
Think of unit productions as 'pointers' - they just redirect to another variable. Replace the pointer with the actual content (non-unit productions) of what it points to.
Use a triangular table where each cell P[i,j] contains variables that can derive substring from position i to j. Build from smaller substrings to larger ones like a Pascal's triangle.

analogies.analogy: CNF is like a restaurant with only two items on the menu: either a single dish (terminal) or a combo of exactly two items (two variables). No single-item sides, no triple combos allowed.
Nullable variables are like 'optional' items in a form - they can be filled in or left blank. When removing them, you must consider all combinations of presence/absence.
Unit productions are like taking a elevator to floor B from floor A, then stairs from B to C. CNF conversion eliminates the elevator - you must go directly from A to C.
Useless symbols are like rooms in a building no one can reach from the entrance. They add complexity without serving any purpose in derivations - remove them!
